To solve the inequality, we will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: x + 8 ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality │x + 8| − 3 ≥ 6 simplifies to (x + 8) − 3 ≥ 6.

Solving this inequality:
x + 5 ≥ 6
x ≥ 6 - 5
x ≥ 1

Case 2: x + 8 < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes │- (x + 8)| − 3 ≥ 6.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (x + 8) - 3 ≥ 6
- x - 11 ≥ 6
- x ≥ 6 + 11
- x ≥ 17

Thus, for this case, x ≥ 17.

Combining both cases, the solution to the inequality │x + 8| − 3 ≥ 6 is x ≥ 1 or x ≥ 17.

To solve the inequality, we will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: x + 8 ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality │x + 8| − 3 ≥ 6 simplifies to (x + 8) − 3 ≥ 6.

Simplifying this inequality:
x + 5 ≥ 6
x ≥ 6 - 5
x ≥ 1

Case 2: x + 8 < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes │- (x + 8)| − 3 ≥ 6.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (x + 8) - 3 ≥ 6
- x ≥ 6 + 3 - 8
- x ≥ 1

Thus, for this case, x ≥ 1.

Combining both cases, the solution to the inequality │x + 8| − 3 ≥ 6 is x ≥ 1.

To solve the inequality, we will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: 1/3x + 3 ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality |1/3x + 3| < 15 simplifies to (1/3x + 3) < 15.

Simplifying this inequality:
1/3x + 3 < 15
1/3x < 15 - 3
1/3x < 12

To isolate x, we can multiply both sides by 3:
(1/3)x(3) < 12(3)
x < 36

Case 2: 1/3x + 3 < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes |- (1/3x + 3)| < 15.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (1/3x + 3) < 15
-1/3x - 3 < 15
-1/3x < 15 + 3
-1/3x < 18

To isolate x, we can multiply both sides by -3, remembering to flip the inequality symbol:
(-1/3)x(-3) > 18(-3) [flipping the inequality symbol]
x > -54

Thus, for this case, x > -54.

Combining both cases, the solution to the inequality |1/3x + 3| < 15 is -54 < x < 36.

To solve the inequality, we will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: 7x + 3 ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality |7x + 3| < 11 simplifies to (7x + 3) < 11.

Simplifying this inequality:
7x + 3 < 11
7x < 11 - 3
7x < 8

To isolate x, we can divide both sides by 7:
(7x)/7 < 8/7
x < 8/7

Case 2: 7x + 3 < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes |- (7x + 3)| < 11.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (7x + 3) < 11
-7x - 3 < 11
-7x < 11 + 3
-7x < 14

To isolate x, we can divide both sides by -7, remembering to flip the inequality symbol:
(-7x)/(-7) > 14/(-7) [flipping the inequality symbol]
x > -2

Thus, for this case, x > -2.

Combining both cases, the solution to the inequality |7x + 3| < 11 is -2 < x < 8/7.

We will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: 19 + x ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality |19 + x| < 2 simplifies to (19 + x) < 2.

Simplifying this inequality:
19 + x < 2
x < 2 - 19
x < -17

Case 2: 19 + x < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes |- (19 + x)| < 2.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (19 + x) < 2
-19 - x < 2
- x < 2 + 19
- x < 21

Thus, for this case, x < 21.

Combining both cases, the solution to the inequality |19 + x| < 2 is x < -17 or x < 21.

﻿
Solve the inequality.
Be sure to simplify your answer.
|6x - 3| -4 > 8

To solve the inequality, we will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: 6x - 3 ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality |6x - 3| - 4 > 8 simplifies to (6x - 3) - 4 > 8.

Simplifying this inequality:
6x - 3 - 4 > 8
6x - 7 > 8

Adding 7 to both sides:
6x - 7 + 7 > 8 + 7
6x > 15

Dividing both sides by 6 (since 6 is positive):
(6x)/6 > 15/6
x > 2.5

Case 2: 6x - 3 < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes |- (6x - 3)| - 4 > 8.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (6x - 3) - 4 > 8
- 6x + 3 - 4 > 8
- 6x - 1 > 8

Adding 1 to both sides:
- 6x - 1 + 1 > 8 + 1
- 6x > 9

Dividing both sides by -6 (since -6 is negative):
(-6x)/(-6) < 9/(-6)
x < -1.5

Thus, for this case, x < -1.5.

Combining both cases, the solution to the inequality |6x - 3| - 4 > 8 is x < -1.5 or x > 2.5.

﻿
Solve the inequality.
Be sure to simplify your answer.
|6x - 3| - 4 > 8

To solve the inequality, we will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: 6x - 3 ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality |6x - 3| - 4 > 8 simplifies to (6x - 3) - 4 > 8.

Simplifying this inequality:
6x - 7 > 8

Adding 7 to both sides:
6x - 7 + 7 > 8 + 7
6x > 15

Dividing both sides by 6:
x > 2.5

Case 2: 6x - 3 < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes |- (6x - 3)| - 4 > 8.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (6x - 3) - 4 > 8
- 6x + 3 - 4 > 8
- 6x - 1 > 8

Adding 1 to both sides:
- 6x - 1 + 1 > 8 + 1
- 6x > 9

Dividing both sides by -6 and flipping the inequality symbol:
(-6x)/(-6) < 9/(-6)
x < -1.5

Thus, the solution to the inequality |6x - 3| - 4 > 8 is x < -1.5 or x > 2.5.

To solve the inequality, we will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: 6x - 7 ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality |6x - 7| > 18 simplifies to (6x - 7) > 18.

Solving this inequality:
6x - 7 > 18
6x > 18 + 7
6x > 25

Dividing both sides by 6:
x > 25/6

Case 2: 6x - 7 < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes |- (6x - 7)| > 18.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (6x - 7) > 18
- 6x + 7 > 18
- 6x > 18 - 7
- 6x > 11

Dividing both sides by -6 and flipping the inequality symbol:
(-6x)/(-6) < 11/(-6)
x < -11/6

Thus, the solution to the inequality |6x - 7| > 18 is x < -11/6 or x > 25/6.

To solve the inequality, we will consider two cases: when the expression inside the absolute value is positive and when it is negative.

Case 1: 1/4x - 1/3 ≥ 0 (expression inside the absolute value is positive)
In this case, the inequality |1/4x - 1/3| ≤ 1/3 simplifies to (1/4x - 1/3) ≤ 1/3.

Simplifying this inequality:
1/4x - 1/3 ≤ 1/3
1/4x ≤ 1/3 + 1/3
1/4x ≤ 2/3

Multiplying both sides by 4/1 (inverting the fraction):
(1/4x)(4/1) ≤ (2/3)(4/1)
x ≤ 8/3

Case 2: 1/4x - 1/3 < 0 (expression inside the absolute value is negative)
In this case, we need to negate the expression inside the absolute value so that the inequality becomes |- (1/4x - 1/3)| ≤ 1/3.

Simplifying this inequality:
- (1/4x - 1/3) ≤ 1/3
-1/4x + 1/3 ≤ 1/3
-1/4x ≤ 0

Multiplying both sides by -4/1 (inverting the fraction) and flipping the inequality symbol:
(-1/4x)(-4/1) ≥ (0)(-4/1)
x ≥ 0

Thus, the solution to the inequality |1/4x - 1/3| ≤ 1/3 is x ≤ 8/3 or x ≥ 0.
